title: Entada planoseminata
taxonomy: Entada planoseminata is a species of legume native to tropical regions. It belongs to the Fabaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Entada planoseminata is further classified into the subfamily Faboideae. This subfamily includes a wide variety of legumes, including many species of trees, shrubs, and vines. Within the subfamily Faboideae, Entada planoseminata is classified into the genus Entada.
uses: Entada planoseminata has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its seeds are said to possess anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating intestinal worms and reducing inflammation. In some traditional medicines, the plant is also used to treat diarrhea, dysentery, and other gastrointestinal problems.
